items.title: Woman struck man with car 'at speed' in Hobart frontyard, police say

children.content: Tasmania Police say a man has been taken to hospital in a serious condition after being struck by a car driven by a woman he knew.

items.title: Greens leader apologises for comparing premier to Russian dictator

children.content: The leader of the Tasmanian Greens has apologised for likening Premier Jeremy Rockliff to Joseph Stalin, the infamous Soviet dictator.

image.altText: Black and while photo of Russian Communist leader, Joseph Stalin, in uniform minus hat.

items.title: Drone footage shows extent of seal deaths on Australian island

children.content: Research has revealed the devastating impact of bird flu on a remote Australian territory where more than 13,000 baby seals are now believed to have been killed by the virus.

items.title: TassieCast: Is Tasmania's forestry debate about to reignite?

children.content: Changes to environmental laws may mean a return to the 'Forest Wars' after a period of what many considered relative peace. Airlie Ward talks with investigative reporter Adam Holmes.

gallery.altText: Production forest being thinned in the Florentine, Tasmania

items.title: AI factory 'brings into doubt' Marinus Link future

children.content: Tasmania's cool climate and access to renewable energy has made it an attractive place for AI data centre investment, but energy industry voices are warning that the business case for the Marinus Link project appears increasingly unviable if power-hungry players such as AI data centres continue to expand across the state.

image.altText: Man stands in front of a large drum of cable.

items.title: Sell-out shows, cult status line-up: How Dark Mofo catapults artists

children.content: Her headline show at Dark Mofo sold out in just over an hour, something Tasmanian Indigenous singer Miss Kaninna says couldn't have happened without the winter festival's ability to attract "massive, massive" music industry names.

image.altText: Miss Kaninna sits in an armchair in a room with red lighting.

items.title: Retiring and moving overseas: Nice idea, but do your research

children.content: With some foreign countries offering cheaper living expenses, it's no surprise that moving overseas to retire is increasingly attractive to some older Australians. But before you up stumps and book that flight, read this.

articles.title: How Socceroo Patrick Beach became the 'last line of defence' at World Cup

items.title: North Queensland reacts to Rinehart's idea of giving islands to Musk
